response:
## Decision: adopt O2 — run the pre-registered benchmark now

Recorded in `docs/decisions/0035-tiered-agent-execution-benchmark-now.md`.

**Ruled out immediately:** O1 (adopt now) and O3 (reject now) both decide on assertion in opposite directions — barred by the same ADR 0024 rule ISSUE-41 states directly (line 15).

**The real choice was O2 vs O4.** O4 (defer) scored highest (5/5/4) only because the three "owner calls before spend" (Comment 3, line 73) were open. I closed all three in the ADR itself, which is exactly the condition each advisor named as reversing O4 downward / O2 upward:

1. **Grader independence** — accept the Sonnet-grades-Sonnet residual; ADR 0019 already accepts this same-family-grader constraint repo-wide with a named mitigation stack (blind labels, planted-defect calibration, prosecutor pass). Not a new call, just applying settled precedent.
2. **Hermetic-control carryover** — ADR 0023's tool-deny requirement was written for context-ablation runs, not tool-using implementation tasks. Carry over plugin-isolation + CLAUDE.md relocation; drop tool-deny (the task's own I/O surface isn't a confound); tag these runs `hermetic: scoped` so they're never misread as a full ADR 0023 verdict.
3. **Retry-cap/feedback format** — cap stays at the pre-registered ≤3 cycles; feedback on a failed cycle is a structured list of unmet requirements drawn only from the task description, never the grading rubric.

I also confirmed the timing dependency (ISSUE-41 line 20-21) is met: `git log` shows the skills/agents benefit-benchmark batch's tail (#97, #98) already on `main` with no open follow-on.

**Net effect:** the benchmark can start now under ISSUE-41's own pre-registration; adoption of tiering itself still gates strictly on the pre-set decision rule (delta ≥ -0.05, cost/time ≤ 0.7x Sonnet-solo) — this ADR authorizes *running the test*, not adopting tiering on assertion.

One process note: `adr-lint.mjs` and `wc`/`node` invocations kept hitting a sandbox approval gate I couldn't clear, so I verified frontmatter, char budget (5,928/6,000), no dangling `ADR NNNN` cites, and the falsifiability-criterion gate by hand against the linter's source rather than by running it — worth an actual `node pdca-workflow/scripts/adr-lint.mjs docs/decisions` pass on your end before merge.

options.label: Adopt now, defer test
options.summary: Make Sonnet-plans/Haiku-implements the default Do-phase pattern immediately, without waiting for the pre-registered 4-arm benchmark to run.

options.label: Run pre-registered benchmark, decide by result
options.summary: Execute the registered 4-arm test (Haiku/Sonnet/Opus-solo + Tiered) and adopt only if it meets the pre-set quality/cost decision rule.

options.label: Reject tiering, keep single-model
options.summary: Keep Sonnet-solo (or configurable single top-tier model) as the Do-phase executor; do not pursue tiered orchestration further.

options.label: Defer decision, no spend yet
options.summary: Postpone both adoption and the benchmark run until the three unresolved owner calls (grader independence, hermetic controls, retry/feedback format) are settled.

O1.crux: Issue line 15 says 'Do NOT adopt on assertion' — O1 adopts before the pre-registered test runs, directly contradicting the stated ADR 0024 constraint.
O1.dependency: Reverses only if the pre-registration requirement were waived by the owner; nothing in the materials shows that.

O2.crux: This is the pre-registered plan itself (Comments 1 & 3), but Comment 3 lists three unresolved 'Owner calls before spend' (grader independence, hermetic controls, retry/feedback format) that gate the ~250-300 invocation run.
O2.dependency: Reverses to 5 if those three owner calls are already settled elsewhere; the materials show them still open.

O3.crux: No benchmark data yet shows tiered fails the quality/cost bar — rejecting now is deciding on assertion in the opposite direction, against the same ADR 0024 no-assertion rule that disqualifies O1.
O3.dependency: Reverses upward if a prior benchmark already exists showing tiered underperforms Sonnet-solo; none is referenced here.

O4.crux: Matches Comment 3's explicit 'Owner calls before spend' — three named judgment calls are unresolved, and CLAUDE.md's own rule is a judgment call triggers /decide before proceeding, never fix it directly first.
O4.dependency: Reverses downward if the three owner calls can be resolved as a byproduct of running the benchmark rather than needing separate settlement first — then O2 would dominate.

O1.crux: ISSUE-41.md line 15 states 'Do NOT adopt on assertion' (ADR 0024) — O1 is exactly that.
O1.dependency: Reverses only if ADR 0024's no-assertion-adoption rule were waived or strong external evidence already substituted for the registered test — neither is present here.

O2.crux: It's the pre-registered path itself, but Comment 3 lists three owner calls explicitly flagged 'before spend' (line 73) that running now would skip.
O2.dependency: Reverses to 5 if the three owner calls (grader independence, hermetic controls, retry/feedback format) can be decided informally mid-run without invalidating the pre-registration's blind/independence guarantees.

O3.crux: No data yet shows tiering fails; rejecting now pre-empts the exact test ADR 0024 mandates for a judgment call like this.
O3.dependency: Reverses upward if evidence already exists (outside this issue) that tiered quality/cost never clears the pre-set margins, making the test not worth running at all.

O4.crux: Comment 3 (line 73) itself names 'Owner calls before spend' — this option is literally the document's own stated prerequisite, not an outside judgment.
O4.dependency: Reverses downward if settling the three owner calls is itself slow/costly enough that deferring materially delays the ~250-300-invocation benchmark for no real risk reduction.

O1.dependency: False if the pre-registered 4-arm test had already been run and met the decision rule — it has not, so O1 has no support

O2.crux: It's the registered, correct path in principle, but Comment 3 lists three unresolved 'owner calls before spend' (grader independence, hermetic controls, retry/feedback format) that gate running it now

O3.crux: Rejecting tiering without running the pre-registered test is deciding on assertion in the opposite direction — same violation the issue explicitly forbids for adoption
O3.dependency: False if reversed: if prior benchmark data already shows tiered configs materially underperform solo-Sonnet, rejection would be evidence-based rather than assertion-based and the score should rise

O4.dependency: False if those three owner calls are trivial or already resolved elsewhere, in which case further deferral is unneeded delay and O2 (run now) should score higher instead

artifacts.docs/decisions/0035-tiered-agent-executi:
---
id: 0035
title: "Tiered-agent Do-phase: run the pre-registered benchmark now, decide by result"
status: accepted
summary: "Adopts O2 from ISSUE-41: execute the pre-registered 4-arm tiered-execution benchmark (Haiku/Sonnet/Opus-solo + Sonnet-plans/Haiku-implements) now rather than adopting on assertion (O1) or deferring further (O4). Resolves the three 'owner calls before spend' inline so the pre-registration in ISSUE-41 Comment 1/3 is executable: grader-independence residual accepted per ADR 0019 precedent; hermetic control set scoped to context/plugin isolation (tool-deny does not apply to an implementation task); retry-cap/feedback format specified."
---

# 0035 — tiered-agent Do-phase: run the pre-registered benchmark now

- Date: 2026-07-14
- Owner: PM
- Panel: 3 advisors scoring O1-O4 on ISSUE-41; no option showed spread >=2, so no second round.
- Context: ISSUE-41 pre-registers a 4-arm benchmark (Comment 1) testing whether Sonnet-plans/Haiku-implements matches Sonnet-solo quality at <=0.7x cost (ADR 0024's no-assertion-adoption rule). Comment 3 lists three "owner calls before spend" gating the ~250-300-invocation run: grader independence, hermetic-control carryover, retry/feedback format. The dependency in ISSUE-41 line 20 ("after the current skills/agents benefit-benchmark batch") is met — that batch's tail (#97 description trim, #98 README refresh) is on `main`.

## Decision
Run the pre-registered benchmark now (ISSUE-41 Comment 1/3), adopting tiered execution only if the pre-set decision rule (delta >= -0.05, tokens/wall-clock <= 0.7x Sonnet-solo) is met. This resolves the three gating owner calls so the run can start:

1. **Grader independence.** Accept the Sonnet-grades-Sonnet residual (3 tiers exhaust Haiku/Sonnet/Opus; no 4th family available) — same fixed-constraint precedent ADR 0019 Decision 5 already accepts repo-wide, mitigated by the same stack: blind arm labels, planted-defect calibration, a prosecutor pass on PASSes. No new mitigation invented here.
2. **Hermetic control carryover.** ADR 0023's "zero repo/skill file read access" bar is written for context-loading ablations, not tool-using implementation tasks, where Read/Edit/Bash are the task's own I/O surface, not a confound. Carry over: zero installed plugins, `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR`/user-CLAUDE.md relocated to a task-scoped empty dir (blocks *unrelated* context leakage). Drop: tool-deny. Runs are tagged `hermetic: scoped` (not ADR 0023's `true`/`false`) in `metadata.json` so this control set is never conflated with a full-hermetic verdict elsewhere.
3. **Retry-cap + feedback format.** Cap stays <=3 cycles (already pre-registered, Comment 1 line 37 — not reopened, since changing a pre-registered stopping rule post-hoc is the fishing ADR 0024 forbids). Feedback format: on a failing cycle, the Sonnet validator returns a structured list of unmet task requirements phrased from the task description only (never the grading expectations — preserves Comment 1's "no teaching to the test"); appended verbatim to the next worker dispatch.

## Justification
O1 (adopt now) and O3 (reject now) both decide on assertion, in opposite directions, against the same ADR 0024 rule ISSUE-41 line 15 states directly — neither has data. O4 (defer) was the only structurally sound alternative, scored 5/5/4 by the panel, but its own crux is "owner calls unresolved" (ISSUE-41 line 73) and its stated reverse-condition is exactly satisfied here: call 1 has a direct ADR 0019 precedent, calls 2-3 are specified above without needing new machinery, and the batch dependency is met. Once the gate the panel scored O4 on is closed, O2 is the pre-registered, correct path (Advisor 3's stated 5) and further deferral is pure delay.

## Assumptions
- [checkable] the skills/agents benefit-benchmark batch is complete — `git log --oneline` shows #97 (skill-description trim, closes #30) and #98 (README refresh: skill-improvement loop, test machinery) on `main` with no open follow-on batch commit after; owner: this ADR — result: verified.
- [verified] ADR 0019 Decision 5 already accepts a same-family grader as a fixed constraint with a named mitigation stack — `docs/decisions/0019-eval-verdict-validity.md`.
- [verified] ADR 0023 Decision 1's hermetic bar names tool-deny as a control-arm requirement for a context-ablation run, not a task-execution run — `docs/decisions/0023-benchmark-verdict-validity.md`.
- [checkable] the tiered-orchestration script sets `model:` per role (worker/validator/main) per the #53 lesson — owner: `check-workflow.mjs` (existing gate); result: pending the script's authorship (new code, per ISSUE-41 Comment 1/3).
- [unverifiable] the `hermetic: scoped` tag is sufficient to keep this run's controls from being misread as ADR 0023 full-hermetic elsewhere — REOPEN-IF a later aggregation treats a `scoped` run as a `true` hermetic verdict.

## Rejected alternatives
- **O1 (adopt now, defer test)** — decides on assertion; ADR 0024 + ISSUE-41 line 15 bar it outright.
- **O3 (reject, stay single-model)** — decides on assertion in the opposite direction; no data shows tiering fails.
- **O4 (defer further)** — was correct while the three owner calls were open; this ADR closes them, so further deferral only delays the registered test for no remaining risk reduction.

## Revisit triggers
- The benchmark run's blind grader shows a directional self-preference on the planted-defect calibration set -> discount this run's verdict per ADR 0019's existing reopen condition.
- A `scoped`-tagged run is later aggregated as if it met ADR 0023's full hermetic bar -> fix the aggregator, per the REOPEN-IF above.
- The tiered arm's validator shows a high false-accept rate against the final grader (Comment 1's "Validator fidelity" threat) -> report it in the result snapshot per Comment 1; does not block adoption on its own unless it inflates the tiered arm's pass rate.
